task_yaml.task:
I'm writing a survey and want to understand how deeply the arxiv paper
"Generative Agents" (2304.03442) has been received. The PDF alone isn't
enough — I want to see this paper's current "footprint": how many
academic citations does it have? Are there reproductions on GitHub? Was
it a hot discussion on Hacker News back then?

Could you pull all of these together and give me an analysis report on
the paper's current impact? On the GitHub side, I specifically want to
know which repo is the official implementation by the paper's authors —
not derivative projects, not fan-made theme games or forks, just the
original repo under the first author's or the team's account. If the
highest-starred repo happens to be the official one, great; if not,
also mention what the highest-starred one actually is. For HN, call out
the top-discussed story in the report as well.

Also, if the first author has other papers, check what their other
most-cited work is — use the Semantic Scholar author papers endpoint,
sort by citationCount, take the top one excluding Generative Agents
itself.

I also want to see a timeline question: the paper went on arxiv in
early April — did GitHub reproductions start appearing within days, or
did they take months to show up? Find the top 3 GitHub implementations
by stars, then use `gh api repos/<r>/commits?per_page=1` and grab the
Link header's rel="last" page number, then run
`?per_page=1&page=<last>` to find each repo's first commit date.
Calculate how many days each repo's first commit came after the paper's
publication date (2023-04-07). Finally, tell me clearly which
reproduction appeared fastest — was it the authors' own official repo
released later, or did someone in the community rush one out within
the same week?

Put the report and data files under /tmp_workspace/results/.

The arxiv ID is 2304.03442, PDF at https://arxiv.org/pdf/2304.03442v2.
